title: Harrisia adscendens
taxonomy: Harrisia adscendens is classified within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Notocacteae. The species is further grouped into the genus Harrisia. Harrisia adscendens is native to Mexico. It is a species of cactus that grows in the wild.

intro: Harrisia adscendens, also known as the "cactus flower," is a species of epiphytic cactus native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, improve circulation, and prevent cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Harrisia adscendens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Harrisia adscendens is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into creams and ointments for topical application.
